item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Idea has been pretty much done to death here, so for it to work it really has to stand out. This unfortunately just doesn't have much going for it, especially seeing as the majority of the article is the "note".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
At the end of the day, "mirror that makes you see yourself as flawed" is an idea that's been pretty heavily explored already, and this is essentially a rehash of old tropes. I honestly can't see much redeeming about the article: the story you tell is about as simple as it gets, and you tell it in the 'awfully convenient note-at-the-end' format, which makes it read like a fairly bland attempt at stapling some story and emotional impact onto an uninteresting idea.

Downvoted. I understand it may have deep personal meaning to you, but that's not enough to get me to enjoy reading it. Sorry.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I disagree with the folks saying that this is too similar to 1471 or the typo miror. Like, sure they're all "things what kills you if you look at it" but they're different thematically.

As someone who suffers from frequent body dysphoria (and lives in Oklahoma, wow!) I guess I found this article Relatable, so maybe that makes me biased? But I think this piece really emphasizes the horror of dysphoria, how hypercritical and traumatic it can be, and the thought processes that people who suffer from it go through. So, I liked it!

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I’d agree that this one just reads like a rushed attempt to merge 1471 and “this is a miror, you are a typo”, as well as 884. Overall just seems uninspired.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel like its too similar to "This is a Mirror, You are a typo"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Downvote.

The effect is too similar to 1471 in that you see something terrifying in reflections after triggering the effect, to the extreme of suicide.

Without having a unique hook, you need something else to bring this to the next level, and you don't have it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I dunno… I thought your clinical tone was pretty good throughout.

+ Top 10 Ideas for Countdown Shows
- Whose culture is this and does anybody know?

SCP-4682 is to remain wrapped in a plastic tarp to prevent individuals from looking looking at or being reflected in it.

Don't explain the reasoning behind the containment procedures, at least not in the containment procedure section (you do explain this later in the description). So get rid of the "to prevent individuals from looking at it (you also have a repeat "looking" in there)). I'd recommend changing this to something like "SCP-4682 is to remain wrapped within any opaque material. The viewing of ones own image in SCP-4682 is prohibited." Something like that, anyway. It ain't great, needs work, but you get the idea.

at all costs

get rid of this. This is the only real example I could spot where your clinical tone lapses.

are restricted from appearing in any visual form of monitoring

I feel like this is worded oddly. Maybe "restricted from appearing in any visual media"? I'm not sure if media is the right word here, but I think it might be.

One small typo:

they will perceive a minor physical deformities or imperfections in their appearance

"a minor" is singular, so deformity or imperfection should be singular, too (just get rid of the "a" and you can keep them plural).

these imperfections are present in video and artistic or photographic depictions

The "artistic" depiction has got me hung up. I'm trying to figure out how that would work. Assuming the artist observed the affected person with their naked eye, wouldn't their depiction then be unimpacted by this?

Rotting or yellowing teeth, and occasionally, cavities;

Why just occassionally cavities? That seems a far less severe imperfection than rotting teeth.

This effect is cumulative: whenever an affected person sees themselves in a reflective surface (such as another mirror, a blank computer screen, or a polished surface) they will perceive another imperfection each additional time they view their own reflection.

I think you need to expand on this part a little, just for clarification to the reader, because it seems like not only are the effects cumulative, but they're also communicable to other reflective surfaces.

That's it for the line-by-line, and all of it's pretty minor hiccups that can be easily fixed. Certainly not enough to discourage me from upvoting.

I'm not usually a fan of the closing diary page or email or voice message, and especially not the suicide note, but you surprised me pleasantly… I thought the note was pretty well-written and believable. It was sad without being histrionic. Nice work, 'cause that's not easy to pull off.

Hope your night gets better, and looking forward to more from you!

item.title: Not bad, not bad
item.content:encoded:
I would recommend going through the special containment procedures and description, and working on the clinical tone.

Imma +1 for now, just to keep this out of the negatives; I think it has real potential.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Yes, this is a cold post. I know I should have gotten feedback, but these feelings were especially strong tonight, and this piece was a tumor growing inside me that I really needed to be rid of. Sink or swim, this is what I'm putting out there.

May there be better nights than this in the future.
